meta.description: Source and prepare music and sound effects

step.name: load_ssd_and_extract_audio_refs
step.action: Load the SSD and extract all music and SFX references from every scene's audio configuration. For each reference, identify: the asset type (music track or sound effect), the reference path or description, timing requirements (start time, duration, fade in/out), volume level, and whether the asset is a direct file reference or a mood/description that needs sourcing.

step.name: check_music_library
step.action: For each music reference: if it specifies a file path, verify the file exists in shared-assets/music/. Check file format (mp3, wav, aac), duration, and metadata (BPM, key, mood tags). If the reference is a mood description without a specific file, search the shared-assets/music/ library for matching tracks by mood, tempo, and genre tags. Present matches for user selection if multiple candidates exist.
step.output: Music availability report with matches and gaps

step.name: check_sfx_library
step.action: For each SFX reference: if it specifies a file path, verify the file exists in shared-assets/sfx/. If the reference is a description (e.g., "whoosh", "click", "ambient rain"), search the shared-assets/sfx/ library for matching effects. Check duration and quality of found effects. If no match is found, flag the SFX as needing manual sourcing or generation.
step.output: SFX availability report with matches and gaps

step.name: flag_missing_items
step.action: Compile a list of all music and SFX items that could not be found or matched in the shared assets library. For each missing item: describe what is needed (mood, duration, style for music; effect type and timing for SFX), suggest potential sources (royalty-free libraries, AI music generation services), and indicate the priority level (P0 if the scene cannot function without it, P1 if a substitute could work).
step.output: Missing items report with sourcing suggestions

step.name: copy_to_episode_assets
step.action: For all found and matched audio assets, copy (or create symbolic links) to the episode's asset directory: episodes/{episode_id}/assets/audio/music/ for music tracks and episodes/{episode_id}/assets/audio/sfx/ for sound effects. Maintain the original file names and add a mapping file that links SSD scene references to the copied asset paths.
step.output: Audio assets copied to episode directory with mapping file

quality-gates.gate: All referenced music files exist in shared-assets/music/ or are flagged as missing
All referenced SFX files exist in shared-assets/sfx/ or are flagged as missing
Found assets are copied or linked to the episode assets directory
Missing items are flagged with descriptions and sourcing suggestions
Audio asset mapping file created linking SSD references to file paths
Audio formats are compatible with the Remotion pipeline
